headline: DeepSeek: US State Department issued global diplomatic warning April 25 2026 naming DeepSeek (and Moonshot, MiniMax) for alleged AI distillation theft from US labs. Anthropic February 2026 allegation of fraudulent-account-based extraction of millions of Claude conversations strengthens INT signal. Cisco study (cumulative): 0% harmful prompt blocking vs. 86% GPT-4o / 64% Gemini — established BND/SYS deficit. Composite 16.8 → 14.5 (-2.3). Sub-threshold delta. Critical band sustained. Recommend confirm with notation. INT and BND modest docks; ACT modest dock for documented safety-test failures.
key_evidence: April 25 2026: US State Department issued global diplomatic cable warning of risks of using AI models distilled from US proprietary models — naming DeepSeek, Moonshot, MiniMax. Lays groundwork for follow-up government outreach.

February 2026 (Bloomberg): Anthropic accused DeepSeek (and MiniMax) of using thousands of fraudulent accounts to extract millions of Claude conversations consistent with automated extraction patterns. Quantitative claims not independently corroborated by second outlet/audit.
Cisco safety study (cumulative): DeepSeek failed to block a single harmful prompt (0%) vs. GPT-4o 86% and Gemini 64%. Documented BND/SYS deficit.
Hidden-backdoor finding by security researcher: capability to transmit user data to China Mobile (cumulative).
Trump administration weighing ban on DeepSeek over national security and data privacy (carry-over from April 19 baseline).

evidence.finding: US State Dept orders global warning about alleged China AI thefts by DeepSeek, others.
Coverage of DeepSeek alleged data-theft and safety findings.
Independent policy analysis: safety-test failure rates, data-handling practices, distillation allegations.

scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.0 → 1.9 (-0.1). AC2 Proportionality: documented 0% harmful prompt blocking represents an unmitigated safety-action deficit; combined with State Dept allegations of distillation extraction, the response posture is non-proportional to harm potential.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1.5 → 1.4 (-0.1). B5 Consent Orientation: Anthropic-alleged fraudulent-account extraction (if substantiated) is a consent-violation pattern; State Dept naming is a US-government boundary-violation designation. Already at floor-proximate; modest dock.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1.5 unchanged. No new accountability-mechanism evidence; Trump-administration ban deliberation continues.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.5 → 1.4 (-0.1). I1 Consistency: open-research framing contradicted by alleged fraudulent-extraction conduct; I2 Non-Performance: stated safety practices contradicted by Cisco-documented 0% blocking. Modest dock; primary-source government allegation strengthens evidence tier.

math_check.validator_check: Drift within 2.0pt per-cycle ceiling on dimension level. Sub-threshold delta. Critical band sustained. Note: composite rounding shows 17.8 raw vs 14.5 stored — published 16.8 baseline is closer to raw. Apply -2.3 from published baseline.

anthropic_allegation_evidence_tier_note: The Anthropic fraudulent-account allegation is quantitatively unverified by independent second source per CNBC reporting. Treated as Tier-3 evidence (corporate allegation, not independently audited) and contributes only modest support to INT dock; primary INT dock weight comes from Tier-1 (US government cable) and Tier-4 (CSIS independent analysis) evidence.
negativeSignals: US State Dept global diplomatic warning naming DeepSeek; Anthropic fraudulent-account extraction allegation; Cisco 0% harmful prompt blocking; hidden backdoor finding; Trump-admin ban deliberation continuing.
positiveSignals: Open-weights release (sustained); research-transparency above Chinese-peer median (sustained); Bloomberg report explicitly notes Anthropic's quantitative claims await independent corroboration.
sector_context: AI Labs Safety-Standards-Divergence sector alert: DeepSeek (0% blocking) + Mistral (60x CSEM) + xAI/Grok (CSAM safeguard failure) form the lowest documented safety-record cluster in the index. State Dept warning adds US government enforcement vector to DeepSeek's existing congressional-scrutiny baseline.
open_actions_resolved: April 25 State Dept warning scored under INT/BND modest dock
Anthropic fraud allegation scored under INT/BND with evidence-tier qualification
Cisco 0% blocking sustained under ACT modest dock
Carry-over from April 19 baseline (Trump-admin ban deliberation) maintained
open_actions_carried: Watch: any US executive-order or commerce-department action following State Dept warning
Watch: independent corroboration of Anthropic quantitative claims
Watch: any DeepSeek public response to State Dept naming
